zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Dorman於1510年出生在英國賀特福爾貝可哈姆斯泰德時，她的父親John Dorman20歲，母親Ann Elizabeth DeKleves20歲。她於1539年5月7日與Thomas Goodale在英國賀特福爾格亞特 貝可哈普斯亞德結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1601年8月8日在英國英格蘭倫敦霍爾波恩聖 安德魯 霍爾波恩 阿波維 馮 巴爾斯 煙雨 聖 喬治 馮 馬迪爾享年91歲。

nameMeanings.name: Dorman
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of   Dearman  . This surname became established in Ireland in the 17th century; it is sometimes found as a variant of   Dornan  .
Americanized form of German Dormann: occupational name for a doorkeeper or gatekeeper or topographic name for someone who lived by the gate of a town or city. Compare Dorer , Dorwart .
Hungarian (Dormán): from the old personal name Dormán.

variants.name: Dearman
meanings: English: from a Middle English personal name Derman, Dorman (Old English Dēormann), meaning either ‘beloved man’ (from dēore ‘dear, beloved’) or ‘fierce, spirited man’ (from dēor ‘wild creature’). See Dear .

Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Ó Duibhdhiorma ‘descendant of Duibhdhiorma’, a personal name meaning ‘black trooper’, see Dormer . There has been some confusion with the more common personal name Diarmaid (see McDermott and compare Dearmond ).

Probably an Americanized form of German Diermann or Thiermann, which derive from short forms of the personal name Dietrich or perhaps from Middle High German tier ‘animal, game’ + man ‘man’ and thus denote a game or venison dealer.

variants.name: Dorer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Oskar.

German: topographic name for someone who lived by the gate of a town or city, from Middle High German tōr(e), Middle Low German dore ‘gate’, + the suffix -(e)r, denoting an inhabitant; alternatively, an occupational name for a gate keeper. See Dorman 2.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Dorman was born in 1510, in Berkhamsted, Hertfordshire, England, her father, John Dorman, was 20 and her mother, Ann Elizabeth DeKleves, was 20. She married Thomas Goodale on 7 May 1539, in Great Berkhampstead, Hertfordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 8 August 1601, in St Andrew Holborn Above the Bars with St George the Martyr, Holborn, London, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 91.

ko.St. Andrew, Holborn, London, England: 영국 잉글랜드 런던 홀본 스트 앤드루 홀본 압브 테 바스 위스 스트 조지 테 마티르
ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Dorman(이)가 1510년 영국 허트퍼드실르 버카스테드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Dorman(은)는 20세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Ann Elizabeth DeKleves(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 1539년 5월 7일 영국 허트퍼드실르 그리트 버카프스테아드에서 Thomas Goodale(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1601년 8월 8일 영국 잉글랜드 런던 홀본 스트 앤드루 홀본 압브 테 바스 위스 스트 조지 테 마티르에서 91세의 나이로 사망했다.

Ela casou-se com Thomas Goodale em 7 de maio de 1539, em Great Berkhampstead, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 2 filhas. ru.St. Andrew, Holborn, London, England: ст андрю холборн абов т барс вит ст джеордж т мартир, холборн, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Dorman родилась в 1510 г. в местности бирхамстед, хиртфордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени John Dorman было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Ann Elizabeth DeKleves, 20. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Goodale 7 мая 1539 г. в местности грит бирхампстид, хиртфордшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 2 дочери. Она умерла 8 августа 1601 г. в местности ст андрю холборн абов т барс вит ст джеордж т мартир, холборн, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 91 год.

es.Warmsworth, South Yorkshire, England: Warmsworth, South Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret Dorman nació en 1510, en Berkhamsted, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, John Dorman, tenía 20 años y su mamá, Ann Elizabeth DeKleves, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Thomas Goodale el 7 de mayo de 1539, en Great Berkhampstead, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 8 de agosto de 1601, en St Andrew Holborn Above the Bars with St George the Martyr, Holborn, London, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 91 años.
